summary: Base64-decoded buffer usage in ts-eslint-helper requires manual review
details: index.js contains Buffer.from(..., 'base64').toString('utf8') decoding patterns at line 7 and an additional Buffer.from at line 86. Base64-decoding to a UTF-8 string is a common obfuscation primitive used to hide hardcoded URLs, payloads, or commands, but it is also used in many legitimate cases (decoding embedded resources, parsing JWT segments, handling binary data). Without traced code corroboration of the decoded content's destination — whether it is passed to eval/Function/exec/spawn or to a network sink — intent cannot be determined from the pattern alone. Manual inspection of index.js around lines 7 and 86 is needed to confirm whether the decoded bytes are executed, exfiltrated, or used benignly.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
